meaning: adj.缺乏的,不足的,将近的,欠缺的v.限制,节省,减少
examples: She began to berate the police for paying scant attention to the theft from her car.
Scant attention has been paid to many of the dubious social and economic assumptions that underlie the plan.
I paid scant attention to what she was saying.
England were down to three frontline bowlers and Gayle showed scant respect for the two fit pacemen.
But at least your penny-and-a-half of profit on the sales dollar comes with a scant dose of capital.
